jokes.text: Water? Like fire water? That's racist, and I do not appreciate it. No, no, no, no, no. I-I didn't mean it like that. I just meant, you know-- I'm just messing with you.
jokes.setup: Ken asks if Leslie meant 'fire water' (alcohol) with apparent offense
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was just messing with her after making her squirm
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that plays on racial sensitivity then subverts it. Ken's deadpan delivery followed by the reveal shows great character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! He's funny. This guy's funny. He does that sometimes.
jokes.setup: Leslie's relieved reaction to realizing Ken was joking
jokes.punchline: Her flustered over-explanation to cover her embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's awkwardness when caught off-guard, but fairly standard reaction beat.

jokes.text: But seriously. I'll have a whiskey.
jokes.setup: After the 'fire water' joke, Ken asks for actual alcohol
jokes.punchline: The ironic twist that he actually does want 'fire water'
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that adds ironic layer to the original joke. Economical and character-consistent.

jokes.text: Murder-y.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing Pawnee's historical relationship with the Wamapoke as 'more...'
jokes.punchline: The casual, made-up word 'murder-y' to describe genocide
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - turning horrific historical reality into a cutesy made-up word. Dark comedy with excellent economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Our slots are downright filthy.
jokes.setup: Ken proudly presenting the casino's new slogan
jokes.punchline: Double entendre playing on 'dirty' slot machines vs sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Decent double entendre but relies on fairly obvious sexual wordplay. Fits Ken's character well.

jokes.text: Why don't we retire to my longhouse and smoke the ceremonial peace pipe to celebrate?
jokes.setup: Ken suggesting they celebrate the deal
jokes.punchline: Overly formal/ceremonial language for what might be casual marijuana use
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy playing on stereotypes vs reality. Ken continues to mess with expectations.

jokes.text: Is he joking? I don't know.
jokes.setup: Ken's peace pipe suggestion leaves them uncertain
jokes.punchline: Their genuine confusion about his intentions
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat. Functional but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: and anything that can be penis-shaped will be penis-shaped.
jokes.setup: Ann listing bachelorette party activities
jokes.punchline: The absurd commitment to penis-shaped everything
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and commitment to the bit. Very Ann character moment - earnest about silly things.

jokes.text: Which is why I'm stress-eating gummy penises.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining her dedication to the party
jokes.punchline: Visual of her literally eating penis-shaped candy while talking
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback that shows Ann's neurotic commitment. The image of stress-eating gummy penises is absurd.

jokes.text: The game is Settlers of Catan.
jokes.setup: Build-up about the guys' crazy bachelor party plans
jokes.punchline: The anticlimatic reveal of a board game
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben moment. Excellent structure with the misdirection. The deadpan delivery after all the bachelor party hype is beautifully anticlimactic.

jokes.text: I mean, I'm nationally ranked, so it isn't even really fair.
jokes.setup: Ben offering to play something else if they don't want board games
jokes.punchline: His casual boast about being nationally ranked at Settlers of Catan
jokes.explanation: Great character detail that escalates the nerdiness. Ben's false modesty about his gaming skills is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Is this gonna be one of those cool bachelorette parties where things get out of control and we murder someone, and then we all have to take a blood oath to never reveal our secret?
jokes.setup: April asking about the type of bachelorette party they're having
jokes.punchline: Her extremely specific and dark fantasy involving murder and blood oaths
jokes.explanation: Peak April - taking innocent party planning and making it sinister. The specificity of the blood oath detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Then I might have to leave early.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying no to the murder scenario
jokes.punchline: April's disappointed reaction suggesting she was genuinely hoping for violence
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that reinforces April's character. Her casual disappointment about no murder is perfectly deadpan.

jokes.text: Don't you ladies look nice... For once?
jokes.setup: Jamm appearing to compliment the women
jokes.punchline: The backhanded 'for once' addition
jokes.explanation: Typical Jamm obnoxiousness. Fits character but is fairly predictable insult humor.

jokes.text: I've actually always been attracted to this one.
jokes.setup: Jamm 'clarifying' his backhanded compliment
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate and vague attraction admission
jokes.explanation: Creepy and uncomfortable, which is the point for Jamm's character. Creates appropriate cringe but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Start drooling, fatties
jokes.setup: Reading the offensive Paunch Burger sign
jokes.punchline: The crude and insensitive advertising slogan
jokes.explanation: Appropriately terrible for Jamm's character and the Paunch Burger brand. Offensive by design but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Their burgers will kill you! They're full of wasps! Yeah, but they taste real good.
jokes.setup: Someone warning about dangerous Paunch Burgers
jokes.punchline: Another person acknowledging the danger but still wanting them
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment. The wasps detail is bizarre, and the citizen's response despite the danger is perfectly Pawnee-citizen logic.

jokes.text: I'm still technically married, but my wife knows the deal.
jokes.setup: Jamm making another inappropriate advance
jokes.punchline: His casual admission of marital problems while hitting on women
jokes.explanation: More Jamm sleaziness. Character-appropriate but not particularly inventive or memorable.

jokes.text: Seven contiguous segments, giving me longest road and a sweet-ass ten points for the win. Oh, booyah!
jokes.setup: Ben playing Settlers of Catan at his bachelor party
jokes.punchline: His intense celebration over board game victory with technical jargon
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben moment combining his nerdiness with unexpected intensity. The technical game language contrasted with 'sweet-ass' is great.

jokes.text: Now, I know you said no strippers 'cause they make you sad.
jokes.setup: Tom acknowledging Ben's anti-stripper stance
jokes.punchline: The unexpectedly sensitive reason (they make him sad)
jokes.explanation: Great character detail that's unexpectedly sweet. The idea that strippers make Ben sad rather than uncomfortable is perfectly him.

jokes.text: Son, there's no wrong way to consume alcohol.
jokes.setup: Tom's explanation of experimental alcohol consumption
jokes.punchline: Ron's straightforward, anti-pretentious response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment. Simple, direct, and completely contrary to Tom's pretentious setup. Very quotable wisdom.

jokes.text: I ordered a beer. That is a beer.
jokes.setup: Ron questioning his beer order at the molecular bar
jokes.punchline: The visual of whatever ridiculous form his 'beer' has taken
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing Ron's confusion with pretentious drink presentation. His deadpan response is perfect.

jokes.text: May I go ahead and chisel your aromasphere?
jokes.setup: Serving the Nimbus Martini
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous made-up terminology for pretentious drink service
jokes.explanation: Great made-up terminology that perfectly captures pretentious mixology culture. 'Aromasphere' is wonderfully ridiculous.

jokes.text: This is the wrong way to consume alcohol.
jokes.setup: Experiencing the molecular bar firsthand
jokes.punchline: Ron's deadpan reversal of his earlier statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Ron has found the exception to his rule. The structure and timing are excellent.

jokes.text: It's a whiskey-infused lotion.
jokes.setup: Serving Ron's Scotch order
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous concept of alcoholic lotion instead of a drink
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd escalation of the molecular mixology concept. The image of Ron receiving lotion instead of whiskey is great.

jokes.text: Can I ask if this entire establishment is a practical joke of some kind?
jokes.setup: Ron's experiences at the molecular bar
jokes.punchline: His polite but pointed question about the bar's legitimacy
jokes.explanation: Great Ron delivery - maintaining politeness while expressing complete bewilderment. Very much in character.

jokes.text: If you don't like the Scotch, they have a vodka that's served in the form of a flash of light.
jokes.setup: Trying to help Ron with drink options
jokes.punchline: The even more ridiculous vodka presentation option
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that pushes the absurdity to its logical extreme. Light-based alcohol is wonderfully ridiculous.

jokes.text: Well, four years of work down the drain, and I have a penis on my head.
jokes.setup: Leslie's dejected state about the park project
jokes.punchline: The additional indignity of wearing penis-themed party gear
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback that adds physical comedy to emotional moment. The juxtaposition works well.

jokes.text: This is why I prefer nightmares.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying her dream is dead
jokes.punchline: April's characteristically dark perspective on dreams vs nightmares
jokes.explanation: Perfect April logic - if dreams can be crushed, nightmares are safer. Dark but oddly logical character moment.

jokes.text: Oh, this is bad. I should not have done this.
jokes.setup: Leslie realizing the consequences of planting artifacts
jokes.punchline: Her immediate regret and panic
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat showing Leslie's conscience kicking in. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Well, if it isn't Babe Lincoln!
jokes.setup: Stripper dressed as Abraham Lincoln arriving
jokes.punchline: Ann's playful nickname combining 'babe' with 'Abe Lincoln'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that works for the stripper scenario. Not particularly deep but functional party humor.

jokes.text: Looks like it's time for the Gettysburg undressed!
jokes.setup: Lincoln stripper preparing his routine
jokes.punchline: Historical pun combining Gettysburg Address with undressing
jokes.explanation: Clever historical pun that works for the absurd scenario. Good wordplay for the stripper character.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I'm just not in the mood for historical nudity.
jokes.setup: Lincoln stripper beginning his routine
jokes.punchline: Leslie's very specific reason for stopping the show
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leslie line. 'Historical nudity' is a perfectly specific and absurd phrase that only she would use in this situation.

jokes.text: I sat behind you in middle school band. Do you still play the clarinet?
jokes.setup: Andy recognizing the Lincoln stripper
jokes.punchline: His innocent question about band during a strip show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - completely missing the point of the situation and focusing on innocent nostalgia. Very much in character.

jokes.text: You never had one before you were married to Wendy? No, it was a green card wedding. I did watch the three-way sex scene from Wild Things a few times the night before, but it didn't really count.
jokes.setup: Discussion about bachelor parties they never had
jokes.punchline: Tom's oddly specific and sad substitute for a bachelor party
jokes.explanation: Good character detail revealing Tom's pathetic past. The specificity of Wild Things makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I didn't even have time to take a shower before my wedding. Or after. For, like, a week and a half.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his rushed wedding to April
jokes.punchline: The escalating grossness of not showering for over a week
jokes.explanation: Good escalating reveal that's perfectly Andy - the extension of not showering makes it grosser and funnier.

jokes.text: Tammy Two thwarted me by calling in a bomb threat to the steakhouse.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining why he never had a bachelor party
jokes.punchline: The extreme lengths Tammy Two went to stop his celebration
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Tammy Two's destructive nature. The bomb threat detail is both shocking and believable for her character.

jokes.text: Boop! That's me hitting the snooze button. Don't talk again for another ten minutes.
jokes.setup: Jerry starting to tell his story
jokes.punchline: Tom's physical gesture and dismissive interruption
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing Tom's dismissiveness toward Jerry. The snooze button metaphor is creative.

jokes.text: and to my bride, Rihanna. We truly did find love in a hopeless place.
jokes.setup: Tom's fantasy bachelor party toast
jokes.punchline: His delusional fantasy about marrying Rihanna with song reference
jokes.explanation: Typical Tom celebrity obsession with pop culture reference. Fits character but is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Shut up, Glenn.
jokes.setup: Glenn trying to participate in the conversation
jokes.punchline: April's blunt dismissal of the stripper
jokes.explanation: Perfect April rudeness. Simple but effective character moment showing her disdain for the situation.

jokes.text: You guys, the penis hats cut your digging time in half. I'm serious. Don't be afraid. Use the penises.
jokes.setup: Ann helping with the artifact digging
jokes.punchline: Her earnest advice about using penis-shaped hats as tools
jokes.explanation: Great callback that finds practical use for the penis hats. Ann's earnest instruction about using penises is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Was she ill, or did your father witness her father committing a crime? Or was she temporarily blind--
jokes.setup: Ron asking how Jerry attracted his wife
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous scenarios for why someone would marry Jerry
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating insults disguised as genuine curiosity. Ron's creativity in finding reasons someone would marry Jerry is brutal.

jokes.text: She was slender. Blonde hair, big breasts, long legs. Ugh, not my type at all.
jokes.setup: Jerry describing what Gayle looked like when they met
jokes.punchline: Claiming conventionally attractive traits aren't his type
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays into the Jerry/Gayle mystery. The 'ugh' sells his supposed distaste for conventional beauty.

jokes.text: He drops it about half the time.
jokes.setup: Worker explaining why he had a backup cone ready
jokes.punchline: Casual confirmation of Jerry's clumsiness being a regular occurrence
jokes.explanation: Good callback that confirms Jerry's reputation extends to local businesses. The matter-of-fact delivery works well.

jokes.text: then I yell at Tom Brady and make him cry, then I hook up with Dave Matthews, and we play golden tee for ten hours.
jokes.setup: Andy describing his perfect bachelor party
jokes.punchline: The bizarre progression from sports to Dave Matthews to arcade games
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy fantasy with random celebrity obsessions and innocent activities. The progression is wonderfully illogical.

jokes.text: I love them like they're my own children. You're big enough to play football yourself, it looks like to me. I can't tell if you're joking, but I hope you're not.
jokes.setup: Andy meeting the Colts owner
jokes.punchline: His earnest response to what might be polite conversation
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his literal interpretation and desperate hope about football compliments.

jokes.text: I got married in your jersey. That's no joke. That's weird. But I respect that.
jokes.setup: Andy sharing personal information with his football hero
jokes.punchline: Reggie Wayne's honest but polite reaction to the weird revelation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Andy oversharing and the player's diplomatic response. Creates appropriate awkwardness.

jokes.text: We're surrounded by hot guys. Boom! Oh, Donna just ripped off a stripper's underwear with her teeth.
jokes.setup: Leslie lying about their party to compete with the men
jokes.punchline: The escalating absurdity of her made-up scenarios
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie's competitive nature and willingness to lie. The Donna detail is appropriately outrageous.

jokes.text: Can I take a break? My hands are tired. Uhuh. We paid for an hour. You gonna dig for an hour.
jokes.setup: Glenn wanting to stop digging artifacts
jokes.punchline: April treating him like hired labor despite him being a stripper
jokes.explanation: Great subversion where the stripper becomes manual labor. April's businesslike attitude about exploitation is perfect.

jokes.text: Gingrich, Gergich. I wonder if we're related.
jokes.setup: Mix-up with restaurant reservation names
jokes.punchline: Jerry's innocent speculation about being related to Newt Gingrich
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry moment showing his obliviousness. The political connection adds mild humor but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: You'd be surprised.
jokes.setup: Waiter saying he doesn't need to specify liquid whiskey
jokes.punchline: Ron's deadpan response referencing their molecular bar experience
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Ron's lingering trauma from the molecular bar. Perfectly economical delivery.

jokes.text: I like her. Whoa! Wow!
jokes.setup: Ron being asked about Diane
jokes.punchline: His rare emotional admission causing shock in his friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment where his minimal emotional expression shocks everyone. Great character development beat.

jokes.text: Japanese, Muslim, Jewish, none of those weird countries.
jokes.setup: Worker explaining what artifacts they haven't found
jokes.punchline: His ignorant categorization of religions as 'weird countries'
jokes.explanation: Creates cringe through ignorance but relies on cheap stereotyping for humor. Fits the character but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Yes, please. Take extra cream, 30 sugars.
jokes.setup: Being offered coffee
jokes.punchline: Leslie's extreme sugar requirements
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie sugar addiction. The specific '30 sugars' number makes it funnier than just saying 'lots.'

jokes.text: I also found some weird-looking gummy candy.
jokes.setup: Worker listing artifacts found at the site
jokes.punchline: Discovery of the gummy penises from the bachelorette party
jokes.explanation: Good callback connecting the two storylines. The innocent description of gummy penises as 'weird candy' works well.

jokes.text: You have turned into a very beautiful, wise, and fertile government employee.
jokes.setup: Leslie praising April for being right
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and inappropriate inclusion of 'fertile'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment where her compliments get weird and inappropriate. The 'fertile' addition is unexpected and funny.

jokes.text: It's kind of weird when she just starts raining compliments on you, huh? Don't try to bond with me.
jokes.setup: April trying to bond with Ann over Leslie's behavior
jokes.punchline: Ann's immediate rejection of any bonding attempt
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ann's wariness of April. The immediate shutdown is appropriately harsh.

jokes.text: Usually you can only get board game t-shirts in XXL.
jokes.setup: Ben commenting on his bachelor party t-shirt
jokes.punchline: His knowledge of board game merchandise demographics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben nerd observation. His specific knowledge of board game shirt sizing reveals deep familiarity with the hobby.

jokes.text: In 1951, Pawnee made it illegal for people to 'Dance or smell like a native American.'
jokes.setup: Leslie listing historical injustices to provide context
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and offensive law about dancing and smelling
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about historical racism that's absurd enough to be funny while still being awful. Good social satire.

jokes.text: He really plays white people like a fiddle. Wow. It's amazing to watch.
jokes.setup: Ken masterfully manipulating the situation
jokes.punchline: April and Ann's admiring commentary on his manipulation skills
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on Ken's character. April and Ann appreciating his manipulation is perfectly in character for both.

jokes.text: That is not great. It sucks.
jokes.setup: Leslie confessing to planting artifacts
jokes.punchline: Ken's blunt escalation from 'not great' to 'sucks'
jokes.explanation: Good Ken deadpan - he starts diplomatic then gets direct. The progression from understated to blunt is well-timed.

jokes.text: Maybe she owns a juice bar in Snerling. Maybe it's April. Maybe I die. Skydiving explosion. Pbbbt.
jokes.setup: Chris fantasizing about his future wife
jokes.punchline: Escalating to death by skydiving explosion
jokes.explanation: Chris's stream-of-consciousness goes from sweet to dark quickly. The sound effect 'Pbbbt' for explosion is endearing.

jokes.text: And I spy on you from my red Corvette.
jokes.setup: Chris's elaborate fake death scenario
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about spying vehicle
jokes.explanation: The red Corvette detail is a nice specific touch in Chris's increasingly elaborate fantasy scenario.

jokes.text: Why, yes, I-- I thought that was obvious.
jokes.setup: Jamm asking if Ken's statement was a threat
jokes.punchline: Ken's matter-of-fact confirmation that it obviously was
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ken moment showing his directness about threatening Jamm. The deadpan delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Does it, white man?
jokes.setup: Jamm saying the headdresses are offensive
jokes.punchline: Ken turning the offense question back on him with racial designation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal where Ken turns the cultural sensitivity question around. The 'white man' designation is perfectly played.

jokes.text: A group of good friends having fun, doing nice things for each other. I mean, that's what's important in life, and I want to write about it.
jokes.setup: Shauna explaining why she wants to write about the bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Her sincere appreciation for wholesome friendship
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment for Shauna showing her genuine appreciation for positive stories. Sets up the Chris meeting nicely.

jokes.text: A group of good friends having fun, doing nice things for each other. I mean, that's what's important in life, and I want to write about it.
jokes.setup: Shauna explaining why she wants to write about the bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Her sincere appreciation for basic human kindness as newsworthy
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that also serves as meta-commentary on the show's values. Works as setup for Chris meeting her.

jokes.text: I have thousands of seconds.
jokes.setup: Shauna asking if he has a second
jokes.punchline: Chris's literally literal and optimistic response about time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment showing his literal interpretation and boundless optimism. The mathematical precision is very him.

jokes.text: How great a day was today? Oh, God, I could die. Greatest day of my life.
jokes.setup: Andy reflecting on meeting the Colts
jokes.punchline: His overwhelming joy expressed as potential death
jokes.explanation: Good Andy enthusiasm callback. His extreme joy consistently expressed through death metaphors shows character consistency.

jokes.text: How great a day was today? Oh, God, I could die. Greatest day of my life.
jokes.setup: Andy reflecting on meeting the Colts players
jokes.punchline: His overwhelming joy escalating to willingness to die happy
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his pure joy and tendency toward dramatic overstatement. Very much in character.
